message: Checks that the deeper design documents beside your `docs/spec/` description are present and well-formed — the guiding principles, the architecture overview with its diagram, and any user guides. It checks structure, not whether the design is right — that is your call and the review lenses'. The full write-up is the default, and a problem only blocks the merge when you have recorded that full write-up: then the guiding principles (`docs/principles.md`) and the architecture overview (`docs/architecture.md`) are expected, each carrying the sections it needs (and the overview a simple diagram), and a gap is a hard finding that names the document and what to add — always self-clearable by writing it or recording that you are keeping the description light. When you have recorded the lighter choice, or your description has no recorded depth yet (an older or hand-authored project), nothing is ever blocked: a missing deeper document is not expected, and a document you did write is only nudged toward good form, never failed — so a project written before this became the default is never retroactively blocked. User guides are always optional — one is checked for shape only when it is present, and an absent guide is never flagged. When your project has no `docs/spec/` description yet, the check says so plainly and does nothing. It only ever reads your files; it never changes them.
